quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Midterm Review Quiz 11
quiz.subtitle: Comprehensive Review — Kinematics through Simple Machines
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: An automated drone descending vertically downward at a constant velocity of $14.5\text{ m/s}$ fires a deceleration thruster at a height of $38.0\text{ m}$ above its landing pad. The thruster creates a uniform upward acceleration that brings the drone to a soft touchdown with a final velocity of exactly zero at the pad surface. Calculate the magnitude of this deceleration.

A geotechnical engineering report details the spatial dimensions of an excavation zone. Convert a recorded cross-sectional area measurement of $0.00760\text{ km}^2$ directly into square centimeters ($\text{cm}^2$).

A heavy metal pendulum bob hangs from a pivot and is pulled to the side, then released from rest. As the bob swings downward from its highest point toward its lowest vertical position, analyze the nature of the work done on the bob by the tension force from the supporting cord.
questions.explanation: Tension always acts toward the pivot, perpendicular to the bob's tangential displacement. $W = Fd\cos90° = 0$.

questions.question: An industrial safety net mechanism utilizes a series of elastic polymer cords to catch falling materials. One cord has an effective stiffness constant of $3,200\text{ N/m}$. Determine the elastic strain energy stored when the cord undergoes a linear stretch displacement of $0.450\text{ m}$ from its relaxed state.

A $0.0650\text{ kg}$ test projectile is fired vertically upward into a thick block of ballistic wax, entering at $180\text{ m/s}$. The wax exerts an average resistive force of $4,800\text{ N}$ to oppose the motion. Use energy principles to calculate the total vertical depth the projectile penetrates into the block before stopping.

A mechanical testing mallet delivers a horizontal blow to a stationary $4.20\text{ kg}$ block on a low-friction tracking rail. The mallet maintains contact for $0.0240\text{ s}$ and delivers an average force of $630\text{ N}$. Calculate the final velocity achieved by the block immediately after the impact.

An electric utility hoist elevates a loaded equipment cage weighing $820\text{ kg}$ vertically through $26.0\text{ m}$ up a transmission tower. The lift requires exactly $45.0\text{ s}$ and the cage moves at uniform speed. Determine the average power output delivered by the hoist system. ($g = 9.80\text{ m/s}^2$)

A high-performance hydraulic actuator draws $1.45 \times 10^5\text{ J}$ of total energy from its primary accumulator system during a heavy mechanical press stroke. Internal fluid resistance and seal friction dissipate $4.64 \times 10^4\text{ J}$ as waste heat. Determine the efficiency percentage of the actuator.

A $34.0\text{ kg}$ robotic testing vehicle moving due west at $5.20\text{ m/s}$ on a frictionless horizontal pad collides head-on with a $16.0\text{ kg}$ vehicle moving due east at $8.50\text{ m/s}$. The $34.0\text{ kg}$ vehicle is brought to a complete rest immediately following the impact. Determine the final velocity of the $16.0\text{ kg}$ vehicle.

A logistics crew uses a heavy-duty inclined plane ramp to slide equipment boxes into a cargo hold. The ramp has a total length of $8.40\text{ m}$ along its slope and climbs to a vertical height of $2.40\text{ m}$. A worker exerts an effort force of $310\text{ N}$ to push a $740\text{ N}$ load up the ramp at constant speed. Calculate the actual mechanical advantage (AMA) of the ramp.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You're well-prepared across all units!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the topics where you made errors.
scoreMessages.50: Keep studying! Focus on pendulum work analysis, km² to cm² conversion, and elastic collisions.
scoreMessages.0: Significant review needed. Revisit kinematics, energy, momentum, and simple machines.
